name: Groundwater Depletion Assessment and Sustainable Management in Semi-Arid India
description: This domain examines the causes, measurement, and management of groundwater depletion across India, drawing on evidence from Granger causality analyses, GRACE-style storage estimates in the Ganga Basin, and studies linking warming temperatures and cropping intensity to aquifer decline. It also covers assessment tools like satellite-based irrigation detection, hydro-economic optimization of crop patterns, and characterization of the subcontinent's groundwater systems and deeper aquifers. Finally, it addresses sustainable governance and community-based solutions, including system dynamics models for village-level water use and farm-pond intensification.

background_concepts.concept: Specific yield / storage coefficient (converting water-table change to storage volume)
background_concepts.why_needed: Groundwater storage-loss estimation papers multiply water-level declines by specific yield without defining this aquifer property; readers need it to understand how depletion volumes are computed.

suggested_resources.title: Unconfined or water table aquifers (specific yield explained)
How to Calculate the Specific Yield of an Aquifer (worked examples)

background_concepts.concept: GRACE satellite gravimetry for groundwater storage anomalies
background_concepts.why_needed: The Ganga Basin storage-loss paper relies on GRACE-derived terrestrial water storage anomalies as one 'line of evidence' but does not explain how gravity measurements yield groundwater estimates.

suggested_resources.title: Water Storage – NASA GRACE-FO Science

background_concepts.concept: Granger causality for time series
background_concepts.why_needed: The groundwater causality readings apply Granger causality to hydrological time series without introducing the statistical concept of predictive causation.

suggested_resources.title: Diffusion / statistics learning resources — Granger causality note (see time-series causality primers)

name: Rainwater Harvesting and Traditional Tank/Watershed Recharge Systems
description: This domain focuses on capturing and recharging water through both engineered and traditional structures, including watershed-based tank systems, percolation tanks, and ancient tank cascade systems in semi-arid Southern India. The readings assess the effectiveness and sustainability of these recharge interventions and identify suitable zones for rainwater harvesting to support agricultural intensification in the semi-arid tropics.

background_concepts.concept: Percolation tanks and managed aquifer recharge
background_concepts.why_needed: Readings critique percolation tanks as recharge structures assuming the reader understands how surface impoundments recharge aquifers via infiltration; the recharge mechanism and specific yield govern effectiveness.

suggested_resources.title: Specific Yield & aquifer recharge relationship

name: Satellite Remote Sensing and ML for Water/Land Feature Mapping
description: This domain covers machine learning methods applied to satellite imagery for mapping water, land, and infrastructure features, spanning object detection (ship detection in SAR, wastewater-treatment monitoring), segmentation, and irrigation-type mapping. It includes tooling and techniques such as OpenMapFlow, data augmentation, attention-based scattering networks, and knowledge-informed deep learning, alongside emerging capabilities like geospatial chain-of-thought reasoning and frame prediction for geostationary imagery.

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation / U-Net encoder-decoder
background_concepts.why_needed: These pipelines assume familiarity with pixel-wise labeling and encoder-decoder segmentation architectures used to extract water and infrastructure features.

suggested_resources.title: U-Net Image Segmentation (PyImageSearch tutorial)

name: Precipitation, Weather, and Climate Forecasting via Deep Learning
description: This domain encompasses deep learning approaches for weather and climate prediction, including precipitation nowcasting, subseasonal and long-range forecasting, temperature prediction, and extreme weather detection using RNNs, graph neural networks, diffusion models, and autoregressive architectures. It also covers benchmark datasets (WeatherBench, RainBench, ClimateNet), climate data generation, uncertainty handling, and self-supervised representation learning for high-dimensional weather and climate data.

background_concepts.concept: Precipitation nowcasting and radar reflectivity
background_concepts.why_needed: Nowcasting readings assume knowledge of radar reflectivity (dBZ) as a proxy for rain rate and the extrapolation framing of short-term forecasting.

suggested_resources.title: Precipitation Nowcasting with Machine Learning (guide)

background_concepts.concept: Diffusion models for image generation
background_concepts.why_needed: PrecipDiff applies image diffusion models to enhance precipitation observations, assuming the forward/reverse denoising process is understood.

suggested_resources.title: A Friendly Introduction to Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models

name: Flood and Drought Risk Modeling and Impact Estimation
description: This domain addresses the modeling of flood and drought risks and their societal impacts, combining hydrological AI models, graph neural network flood prediction, and multimodal machine learning approaches. It emphasizes human and infrastructure dimensions, including damage assessment, mobility patterns, socially informed drought estimation, and flood exposure in vulnerable regions like Bangladesh, as well as decision-support and monitoring systems for flood response.

background_concepts.concept: SAR-based flood exposure detection
background_concepts.why_needed: The Bangladesh flood-exposure study uses satellite-derived submersion measures whose all-weather radar basis is assumed rather than explained.

suggested_resources.title: Flood mapping with Synthetic Aperture Radar (EO4GEO course)

name: Climate Resilience Tools and Participatory Vulnerability Assessment for Rural Livelihoods
description: This domain focuses on participatory tools and frameworks for assessing climate vulnerability and building resilience in rural communities, including CoDriVE, Commons Connect, and the CRISP-M tool for MGNREGA planning. It covers community consultation on natural resource management, retrospective resilience assessment of semi-arid farming systems, and field testing of digital tools for water security, linking pro-poor social protection policies to climate adaptation.

background_concepts.concept: MGNREGA (rural employment guarantee and its planning process)
background_concepts.why_needed: CRISP-M and pro-poor climate readings integrate climate info into MGNREGA planning, assuming readers know this demand-driven, Gram-Panchayat-based social protection scheme.

suggested_resources.title: Explained: MGNREGA (rights-based rural employment scheme)
India: MGNREGS (Global Alliance against Hunger and Poverty)

name: Nature-Based Solutions and Landscape/Watershed Ecological Planning
description: This domain explores ecological and systems-based approaches to landscape and watershed planning, including watershed integrity assessment, landscape ecology metrics, and system dynamics modeling of irrigation efficiency and hydrologic-economic effects. It also examines mental models for water sharing in micro-irrigation, ecological economics modeling, and the broader value and limits of nature-based solutions for addressing climate change and degraded Indian landscapes.

name: AI Flood Mapping and Forecasting from Satellite Data
description: This domain applies AI to detect and map floods from satellite imagery, leveraging Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical data, foundation models, semi-supervised learning, and diffusion-based SAR-to-EO translation. It includes large-scale surface water datasets, flood prediction in sparse-observation settings like Kenya, crowdsourcing-enhanced maps, and analyses of human impacts such as mobility and destruction of populated sites during flooding in Nigeria.

background_concepts.concept: SAR imaging and speckle for flood extent
background_concepts.why_needed: Flood extent mapping readings rely on radar backscatter and elevation-guided cues without explaining SAR imaging principles or speckle noise.

suggested_resources.title: Flood mapping with Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) — FSU Jena course

name: Streamflow and Hydrological Modeling with ML
description: This domain covers machine learning methods for predicting streamflow and runoff, including LSTMs, transformers, graph neural networks, and vision transformers, often applied in data-sparse or data-scarce regions such as Benin and the Ansongo-Niamey Basin. It emphasizes techniques like domain adaptation, fine-tuning global models with local data, missing-data imputation, and theory-guided or physics-informed learning for flood, drought, and flash flood forecasting.

background_concepts.concept: Rainfall-runoff process and streamflow/discharge
background_concepts.why_needed: Streamflow-prediction readings assume understanding of the hydrologic cycle, catchments, and how precipitation becomes discharge.

suggested_resources.title: USGS Precipitation-Runoff Modeling System (PRMS)
Rainfall-Runoff Modeling overview (ScienceDirect Topics)

name: Remote Sensing Foundation Models and Segmentation Methods
description: This domain focuses on foundation models and advanced segmentation techniques for remote sensing, including lightweight pretrained transformers for timeseries, visual prompt-tuning, and adaptations of the Segment Anything Model for change detection. It also covers few-shot instance segmentation, unsupervised change detection for disaster management, geographic location encoders, and machine learning toolkits for Earth system data cubes and coastline delineation.

background_concepts.concept: Foundation models / pretraining and transfer learning
background_concepts.why_needed: These readings assume familiarity with the pretrain-then-finetune paradigm and why it helps data-scarce EO tasks.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Earth Foundation Models (IGARSS 2025 tutorial)
Introducing Geospatial Foundation Models in ArcGIS (Esri)

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation and the U-Net architecture
background_concepts.why_needed: Segmentation and change-detection readings assume encoder-decoder segmentation networks and pixel-level labeling are understood.

suggested_resources.title: Satellite imagery segmentation using U-Net (tutorial)

name: Tropical Cyclone and Extreme Storm Prediction
description: This domain focuses on applying artificial intelligence and generative models to forecast tropical cyclones and their associated hazards. Readings cover techniques such as conditional denoising diffusion models for estimating atmospheric variables from typhoon satellite images, Pix2Pix GANs for wind field diagnosis, and video diffusion models for improved cyclone forecasting. It also addresses 3D cloud reconstruction and enhancing the spatial accuracy of extreme cyclone rainfall in reanalysis data using deep learning.

background_concepts.concept: Conditional GANs / image-to-image translation
background_concepts.why_needed: The Pix2Pix cyclone wind-field paper assumes understanding of paired image-to-image translation with GANs, closely related to U-Net generators.

suggested_resources.title: U-Net Image Segmentation (encoder-decoder, Pix2Pix generator basis)

name: Coastal Change and Shoreline Forecasting
description: This domain examines deep learning approaches for predicting coastal flooding and long-term shoreline evolution under climate change. Readings include climate adaptation-aware and vision-based frameworks for coastal flood prediction that account for shoreline adaptations, as well as forecasting global coastline evolution directly from satellite imagery.

background_concepts.concept: Shoreline delineation from satellite imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: Coastline-forecasting readings assume knowledge of how shorelines are extracted from optical imagery (water/land segmentation).

suggested_resources.title: Satellite image segmentation using U-Net for shoreline delineation (overview)

name: Climate Downscaling and Simulation Correction
description: This domain addresses machine learning methods for correcting biases, downscaling, and emulating climate and Earth system simulations. Readings cover adaptive bias correction for subseasonal forecasting, contrastive learning and image-to-image translation for debiasing and super-resolution, and diffusion-based emulation and downscaling for extreme-event simulation. Additional topics include neural representations for ocean forecasting, radiative transfer modeling, ice flow prediction, and reconstructing land surface boundary conditions.

background_concepts.concept: Statistical vs. dynamical downscaling and GCMs
background_concepts.why_needed: Downscaling readings assume understanding of coarse-resolution GCM outputs and the predictor-predictand statistical relationship.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Model Downscaling (NOAA GFDL)
Statistical downscaling key term (Fiveable Intro to Climate Science)

name: Compound Climate Extremes and Disaster Hazard Prediction
description: This domain covers the detection and prediction of concurrent and interacting climate hazards such as wildfires, droughts, and heatwaves using spatiotemporal deep learning and remote sensing. Readings include burned area mapping and short-term fire hazard prediction, identifying atmospheric drivers of drought and heat, and predicting the concurrence of heatwaves, droughts, and wildfires. It also spans agent-based climate risk modeling, vision transformers for early climate signal detection, and lightweight networks for extreme weather detection.

background_concepts.concept: Compound / cascading climate extremes
background_concepts.why_needed: The concurrence reading assumes the notion of co-occurring hazards (heatwave-drought-wildfire) as an established climate-science concept.

suggested_resources.title: GFDL climate modeling / extremes overview

name: AI for Climate Policy and Ecosystem Valuation
description: This domain explores AI applications for informing climate policy, valuing ecosystems, and supporting environmental and climate justice. Readings include causal frameworks for ecosystem valuation such as wetland flood mitigation benefits, emulating integrated assessment models, and making climate AI systems past- and future-aware for policy evaluation. It further covers carbon footprint perception, real-time emission management in Kenya, environmental justice policy in Atlanta, climate knowledge bases, and explainable geospatial predictions for climate finance.

background_concepts.concept: Integrated Assessment Models (IAMs) and Social Cost of Carbon
background_concepts.why_needed: ML-IAM and climate-policy readings assume the reader knows what IAMs are and how they couple climate and economic systems to compute policy costs.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to integrated assessment modeling of climate change (ScienceDirect chapter overview)
Inside the Integrated Assessment Models: Four Issues in Climate Economics (SEI)

name: Drought Forecasting and Impact Monitoring
description: This domain focuses on forecasting drought severity and duration and monitoring its impacts using deep learning across diverse climate zones. Readings include dedicated datasets like DroughtSet and DroughtED, hybrid recurrent networks, specialized loss functions such as SQUASH, and interpretable methods for extreme-event detection. It also emphasizes localized and socially grounded applications, including Indigenous knowledge-aware monitoring, support for pastoral communities in Northern Kenya, and Twitter-based impact recognition.

background_concepts.concept: Sub-seasonal to seasonal (S2S) prediction
background_concepts.why_needed: The S2S extremes reading assumes familiarity with the forecast horizon between weather and climate timescales.

suggested_resources.title: Fiveable Intro to Climate Science (downscaling/prediction terms)

name: Weather and Climate Model Emulation and Downscaling
description: This domain covers machine learning models that emulate, calibrate, and downscale weather and climate simulations. Readings include benchmark datasets for meteorological downscaling, foundation models such as ClimaX, and emulation of local-scale UK climate models. It also addresses uncertainty quantification and calibration in long-range forecasting with large neural weather models.

background_concepts.concept: Bias correction and super-resolution of meteorological fields
background_concepts.why_needed: The downscaling benchmark assumes understanding of coarse-to-fine mapping and bias correction of model outputs.

suggested_resources.title: Climate Model Downscaling (EPRI Climate Data User Guide)

name: Extreme Weather Event Detection and Forecasting
description: This domain addresses identifying and forecasting extreme weather and hydrological events under a changing climate. Readings include Bayesian inference of severe hail in Australia, lightweight neural networks for improved extreme weather detection, and machine learning approaches for identifying hydrological extremes under global climate change.

background_concepts.concept: ERA5 reanalysis fields as inputs for event detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Detection models are trained on reanalysis meteorological fields, assuming the reader understands their provenance and resolution.

suggested_resources.title: ERA5 atmospheric reanalysis (UCAR Climate Data Guide)

name: Satellite Land Cover and Water Body Segmentation
description: This domain focuses on segmentation and classification of land cover, land use, and water bodies from satellite and aerial imagery using deep learning. Readings cover large-scale surface water detection, wetland delineation with imbalanced segmentation, aquaculture pond mapping, irrigation detection and method mapping, and solar plant mapping. It also spans methodological advances including data augmentation, few-shot transfer learning, self-supervised and semi-supervised methods, contextual and geographical priors, and scalable cloud-based LULC classification.

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation for water body detection
background_concepts.why_needed: Surface-water detection datasets assume the reader knows pixel-wise semantic segmentation and evaluation metrics like mean IoU.

suggested_resources.title: Semantic segmentation with U-Net (overview and IoU/Dice metrics)

name: Social Media and Crowdsourced Disaster Response Analytics
description: This domain examines the use of social media, crowdsourced, and citizen-reported data for understanding and responding to climate disasters. Readings include analyzing Twitter discourse around Cyclone Amphan, improving flood maps through crowdsourcing and semantic segmentation, and recognizing drought impacts from Twitter data and drought impact reports using deep learning and XGBoost.

name: Flood & Disaster Risk Mapping and Response Systems
description: This domain examines technological systems for assessing, monitoring, and responding to disasters, including satellite-based damage assessment and risk monitoring. It covers specialized infrastructure such as nationwide lightning detection networks for weather forecasting in the Philippines, as well as broader systems architecture approaches to disaster risk reduction.

background_concepts.concept: Systems architecture / model-based systems engineering for disaster response
background_concepts.why_needed: The DRR systems-architecture paper assumes familiarity with formal systems-engineering practice for designing interoperable response systems.

suggested_resources.title: Systems Engineering Fundamentals (Defense Acquisition University guide)

name: Rural Water Supply Infrastructure Monitoring & Operations
description: This domain focuses on tracking the functionality and usage of rural water infrastructure, exemplified by electronic sensors deployed in Plateau State, Nigeria. It also addresses digital mapping platforms, such as community water system mapping, designed to support the operators who manage rural water systems.

name: Drinking Water Quality, Safety & Contamination Interventions
description: This domain addresses drinking water safety through interventions targeting contamination, including arsenic-safe water technology and willingness-to-pay studies in West Bengal, India. It also covers technological solutions like air-to-water generators in Kerala and investigations into urban underground water contamination.

background_concepts.concept: Willingness-to-pay / contingent valuation method
background_concepts.why_needed: The arsenic-safe-water study uses WTP survey methods to value a non-market good, assuming familiarity with the stated-preference/contingent valuation approach.

suggested_resources.title: Applications of the contingent valuation method in developing countries (FAO)

name: Community-Based & Indigenous Knowledge Climate Resilience
description: This domain explores how communities and indigenous knowledge contribute to climate resilience, including profiling disaster-stricken communities and digitizing indigenous knowledge. It emphasizes participatory and co-design approaches, such as empowering Bangladeshi communities on water safety and co-designing microclimate sensors.

name: Rainwater Harvesting & Off-Grid Water/Agriculture Systems
description: This domain covers water conservation and rainwater collection technologies for sustainability, with case studies in Guatemala and Northern India. It examines the agricultural impact of farm ponds and the integration of rainwater harvesting with automated off-grid irrigation for food and water security.

name: Gender, Empowerment & Marginalization in Water Programs
description: This domain examines the intersection of gender empowerment and water-related programs, including vetiver grass technology for river restoration and women's empowerment in rural India. It highlights training rural women to monitor water quality and integrating agriculture with clean energy to enhance livelihoods and WASH empowerment.

background_concepts.concept: WASH (Water, Sanitation and Hygiene) framework
background_concepts.why_needed: The women's water-quality monitoring paper frames its impact in terms of WASH empowerment, an institutional/programmatic acronym used without definition.

suggested_resources.title: WASH (Water, Sanitation and Hygiene) — UNICEF

name: Satellite-Derived Socio-Economic & Vulnerability Mapping
description: This domain uses satellite imagery and remote sensing to map socio-economic indicators, poverty, and vulnerability at high resolution. It covers methods including point-to-region co-learning, temporal prediction, time series analysis, and nighttime light reconstruction, with applications spanning Africa, rural India, and urban disadvantaged populations in the United States.

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as an economic-activity proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Poverty-mapping readings use nighttime lights and daytime imagery as wealth proxies, assuming readers understand this remote-sensing-to-socioeconomics link.

suggested_resources.title: Daytime and Nighttime Remote Sensing Imagery Data (World Bank IEG)

name: Satellite Imagery Analysis for Environmental and Socio-economic Monitoring
description: This domain applies deep learning and machine learning to satellite imagery for environmental monitoring, including landslide detection, glacier monitoring, soil carbon estimation, and vegetation greenness forecasting. It also addresses methodological challenges such as domain gaps, interpretability, and data augmentation, with studies spanning Lima, the Hindu Kush Himalaya, India, and Karachi.

background_concepts.concept: Vegetation greenness / spectral vegetation indices (e.g., NDVI)
background_concepts.why_needed: Vegetation-greenness forecasting papers use satellite-derived greenness indices as targets, assuming the reader knows how multispectral reflectance encodes vegetation health.

suggested_resources.title: An Introduction to SAR and Its Applications / Fundamentals of Remote Sensing (NASA ARSET)

name: Computational Hydrology and Terrain Analysis Tooling
description: This domain focuses on computational tools and algorithms for analyzing terrain and hydrological features, including deep learning for geomorphological mapping and gully erosion susceptibility. It covers topographic position and landform analysis as well as GPU-accelerated hydrology algorithms for flow accumulation, watershed delineation, and runoff simulation.

background_concepts.concept: Topographic Position Index and DEM-based landform classification
background_concepts.why_needed: Terrain-analysis readings assume familiarity with digital elevation models and derived indices (TPI, slope, flow) used to classify landforms and erosion susceptibility.

suggested_resources.title: Specific yield / hydrogeology terrain concepts primer
